# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Functions with Managed Identity. Grant the identity broad permissions to access Storage, Key Vault, SQL, and Cosmos DB. Use built-in roles with wildcards for flexibility as we add features. This is for our serverless microservices architecture.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

rbacSetupDefinitionName: '<your_unique_name>' # Define a unique name, e.g., rbac-setup 
locationToDeployResources: <region|country/state> 
roleDefinitionsResourceGroupNameSuffix: '_roledefs'   
subscriptionIdParameterKeyName : '<your parameter key here for subscription id>',   # Add your own parameters if required. You can get it using az account show command in the terminal or Azure Portal  ->  https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/account?view=azure-cli#az_account_show
roleDefinitionResourceGroupName: '<your resource group name>'   # Add your unique Resource Group Name where you want to set roles here   
identityNameParameterKey : "{name}.identity"  // e.g., UserAssignedManagedIdentityForNetworking,UserAssignedManagedIdentityStorageAccounts etc... You can get it using az identity list command ->  https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/identity?view=azure-cli#az_identity
assignRoleDefinitionPermissions:  //Define permissions here e.g., "*", [array of Permission] etc...   For example, '["Storage.Create"]' or all permssions ['*"'] and also you can define wildcard like * which means full access to the resource type in question
permissionAssignmentName: <your_unique name> //Define unique Name for assignment  e.g., assignRoleDefinitionPermissions-Permission   You should use this pattern if adding more permissions or roles within resources after defining a base role definition and permission setup (e.g."assignCustomRoleToUser")
